identity.name: nav-item
identity.summary: What the header nav item on test.synapse.sh actually does today. Use this contract to verify that a consumer build reproduces production behavior pixel-for-pixel. Known design gaps are listed in nav-item.findings.md and tracked in nav-item.contract.aspirational.json.

source.comment: Auto-promoted from evidence/contracts/nav-item/2026-05-20T03-13-12-590Z.probe.json. Layout/typography/states/motion are verbatim from the live crawl. Anatomy, composition, anti-patterns are added by hand (the crawler cannot infer slot intent).

anatomy.comment: Icon and indicator slots exist in the schema but the production nav-item currently uses label only. Icon-only header buttons (logo, docs, settings) are a separate component.

typography.family: "Pretendard Variable", ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif, "Apple Color Emoji", "Segoe UI Emoji", "Segoe UI Symbol", "Noto Color Emoji"

selected.comment: Production currently encodes selected state by color shift only. This is flagged in nav-item.findings.md as gap #2.

current.comment: Production uses Vue Router's .router-link-active class instead of aria-current. Gap #4 in nav-item.findings.md.

antiPatterns: Encoding selected state with color alone (#5D6C83 → #182134). Color-blind users cannot distinguish the active page. See findings #2.
Browser-default focus outline (1px auto rgb(0,95,204)). Not a design-system token. See findings #5.
No transition on state change — instantaneous color flip looks abrupt. See findings #3.
No aria-current attribute — screen readers cannot announce the active page. See findings #4.
